summary: A balanced four-day Kyoto trip focused on food, temples, and scenic neighborhoods. From the vermilion corridors of Fushimi Inari to the bamboo whispers of Arashiyama, this itinerary blends iconic landmarks with hidden local gems at a moderate budget.

items.title: Fushimi Inari Shrine
items.description: Walk the thousands of vermilion torii gates up the mountainside. Arrive by 7 AM to experience the trail in near-solitude.

items.title: Nishiki Market Street Food
items.description: Kyoto's 400-year-old kitchen. Graze on tamagoyaki, mochi, pickles, and fresh tofu from dozens of stalls.

items.title: Kiyomizu-dera Temple
items.description: Iconic hilltop temple with a massive wooden stage overlooking the city. Walk through charming Higashiyama lanes on the approach.

items.title: Sannen-zaka and Ninen-zaka Lanes
items.description: Atmospheric stone-paved streets lined with traditional shops, tea houses, and craft stores.

items.title: Pontocho Alley Dinner
items.description: Narrow atmospheric alley along the Kamo River. Try obanzai — Kyoto-style home cooking served as small seasonal plates.

items.title: Gion Evening Walk
items.description: Stroll through Kyoto's geisha district at dusk. Walk Hanami-koji street — you may spot a maiko heading to an appointment.

items.title: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove
items.description: Walk through towering bamboo at sunrise before the crowds. The rustling canopy overhead is mesmerizing.

items.title: Tenryu-ji Temple Garden
items.description: UNESCO World Heritage Zen temple with one of Japan's finest landscape gardens backed by the Arashiyama mountains.

items.title: Yudofu at Sagano
items.description: Kyoto's signature tofu cuisine — silky hot tofu in simple dashi broth with seasonal vegetables.

items.title: Monkey Park Iwatayama
items.description: Hilltop park with wild macaques roaming freely. Panoramic views of Kyoto from the summit.

items.title: Togetsukyo Bridge and River Walk
items.description: Cross the iconic Moon Crossing Bridge and walk along the Katsura River. Rent a boat if the weather is good.

items.title: Kaiseki Dinner at Gion Nanba
items.description: Refined multi-course traditional dinner featuring seasonal Kyoto ingredients. A splurge-worthy cultural experience.

items.title: Kinkaku-ji (Golden Pavilion)
items.description: The gold-leaf pavilion reflected in its mirror pond is one of Japan's most iconic images. Go early for the best light.

items.title: Ryoan-ji Rock Garden
items.description: Japan's most famous Zen rock garden — 15 stones in raked gravel. Sit on the wooden veranda and contemplate.

items.title: Ramen at Ippudo Kyoto
items.description: Rich tonkotsu ramen from one of Japan's best chains. The Kyoto location offers a regional seasonal special.

items.title: Philosopher's Path Walk
items.description: Peaceful two-kilometer canal-side path lined with cherry and maple trees connecting Ginkaku-ji to Nanzen-ji.

items.title: Nanzen-ji Temple and Aqueduct
items.description: Massive Zen temple complex with a striking brick aqueduct. The views from the Sanmon gate are worth the climb.

items.title: Izakaya Hopping in Kiyamachi
items.description: Bar-hop along the Kiyamachi canal. Try yakitori, edamame, and local craft beer across two or three cozy spots.

items.title: Yasaka Shrine Night Visit
items.description: The illuminated shrine at the edge of Gion is atmospheric at night. Free entry and a quiet close to the day.

items.title: Uji Tea Town Day Trip
items.description: Short train ride to Japan's tea capital. Visit Byodo-in Temple and taste premium matcha at the source.

items.title: Matcha Tasting at Nakamura Tokichi
items.description: Centuries-old tea house serving matcha parfaits, green tea soba, and freshly whisked ceremonial matcha.

items.title: Soba Lunch at Honke Owariya
items.description: One of Kyoto's oldest restaurants, operating since 1465. Order the hourai soba — five tiers of buckwheat noodles served in lacquerware.

items.title: Kyoto Handicraft Center
items.description: Browse and buy traditional crafts — ceramics, fans, textiles, and woodblock prints. You can try hands-on workshops.

items.title: Tea Ceremony Experience
items.description: Participate in a traditional 45-minute tea ceremony led by an English-speaking host in a tatami room.

items.title: Farewell Tempura at Yoshikawa
items.description: Sit at the counter and watch the chef prepare delicate seasonal tempura piece by piece. A memorable final dinner.

items.title: Kamo River Sunset
items.description: End your trip sitting on the banks of the Kamo River, watching the sunset paint the water golden. Pure Kyoto magic.
